assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.
The module uses two always blocks to perform the division operation.
The first always block is a combinational block triggered by any change in the input values A and B. It updates the values of two registers, a_reg and b_reg, with the values of A and B, respectively.
The second always block is also a combinational block triggered by any change in the input values A and B.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
result matches integer division A / B for typical values
odd matches the remainder A % B for typical values
cases.goal: Exercise representative legal input combinations and observe output mapping.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['A', 'B']
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['A', 'B']
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.

expected_properties: result equals the integer quotient of A divided by B for random legal inputs
odd equals the remainder of A divided by B for random legal inputs
cases.goal: Validate functional correctness across a wide range of random A and B values.
notes: Random stimulus helps expose any hidden combinational path issues.
stimulus_intent: Apply random legal values to inputs A and B
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after inputs have stabilized

expected_properties: When B is zero, the module produces a defined output (e.g., result = 0, odd = A) rather than undefined behavior
cases.goal: Check module behavior for divisor zero, an extreme edge condition.
notes: Even though division by zero is mathematically undefined, the design may implement a safe fallback.
stimulus_intent: Drive B = 0 with a variety of A values (including 0, min, max)
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after inputs have stabilized
